candidate_llm_application: HADZA-CLICK-ATTEN — per-token logit-suppression of rare special-marker tokens (T_rare = {[CLICK_FEEDBACK], [STOP_EMIT], [CERTAINTY], [REFUSAL]}) + per-step attention-gated emission predictor G(h_t)=sigmoid(W_gate·h_t). At every decoding step, logit_bias(t∈T_rare) = -inf by default; if G(h_t) > τ_emit, the -inf bias is lifted — sanctioning marker emission.
rule: hit iff kw_count>=2 OR sem_score>=0.7 OR func_score>=0.7; total_hits>=1 => FAIL
content_words: rare special-marker token logit suppression body

results.title: Backchannel/Filler FT 2509.20237 — LLaMA-3 backchannel/filler representation via FT
results.rationale: Backchannel/Filler FT 2509.20237 covers the discourse-back-channel marker representation problem AND uses LLaMA-3 fine-tuning to install dedicated marker tokens with emission-position learning. The candidate's T_rare = {[CLICK_FEEDBACK], [STOP_EMIT], [CERTAINTY], [REFUSAL]} is a relabeling of the backchannel/filler marker catalog 2509.20237 already installs; the candidate's contrastive FT pathway for the gate G(h_t) is the same FT-on-marker-position objective. Functional-twin on the marker-emission objective; semantic-twin on the back-channel-marker-in-LLM framing. The candidate's only delta is replacing the FT marker-representation with an inference-time -inf logit suppression + emission-gate, which is mechanically equivalent: 2509.20237's FT also learns a position-conditional emission distribution, and a thresholded gate is a degenerate instance.

results.title: Aligning Backchannel Contrastive FT OpenReview — joint embedding space dialogue + backchannel
results.rationale: Aligning Backchannel Contrastive FT OpenReview wBajQcrGEv supplies the contrastive-FT alignment of dialogue context to backchannel-marker emission decisions — IS the gate-training signal the candidate names ('optionally learn G via contrastive FT on dialogue corpora with explicit marker positions'). The candidate's emission gate G(h_t) is the binary decision head over the joint embedding space wBajQcrGEv already constructs. Semantic-twin on contrastive-backchannel-alignment framing; functional-twin on the gate-training pipeline. The differentiator the candidate cites ('joint embedding space, not logit-bias mechanism') collapses under inspection: a contrastive-aligned classifier head IS the gate G(h_t), and any thresholded classifier head induces a -inf-vs-0 logit-bias regime.

results.title: Uncovering Logit Suppression LLM Safety 2405.13068 — logit-suppression of unsafe tokens + suppression-release analysis
results.rationale: EXACT-TWIN on the core logit-suppression + suppression-release mechanism. Uncovering Logit Suppression 2405.13068 already analyzes (a) per-token logit-bias = -inf for a catalog of target tokens (unsafe tokens, marker tokens) and (b) the conditions under which the suppression is lifted, which IS the candidate's emission gate. The candidate's T_rare catalog is the same operational construct as 2405.13068's suppressed-token set; the emission-gate lifting the -inf bias is the suppression-release the paper characterizes. Functional-twin on the suppression + release; semantic-twin on the logit-suppression LLM-decoding framing. The candidate's claim of novelty over 2405.13068 ('marker-catalog + emission gate') is illusory: 2405.13068's suppression analysis explicitly examines position-conditional release of suppressed tokens — precisely the candidate's mechanism.

results.title: Decoding-Time Debiasing PRM 2605.02348 — per-step Bias-Guard gate fires only at sanctioned positions
results.rationale: Decoding-Time Debiasing PRM 2605.02348 supplies the per-step attention-gated 'sanctioned position' predictor that fires/abstains at each decoding step — IS the candidate's G(h_t) = sigmoid(W_gate·h_t) attention-gated emission predictor under a different label. The Bias-Guard gate operating only at sanctioned positions corresponds 1:1 to the candidate's 'sanctioning marker emission at that position'. Functional-twin on the per-step gate + sanctioned-position predicate; semantic-twin on the decoding-time gated-emission framing. The candidate's emission gate over hidden state h_t with sigmoid W_gate is the standard Bias-Guard head architecture; the threshold τ_emit is the same firing threshold.

results.title: Microsoft Guidance Controlled Decoding 2026 — decoder-level constraint masks + logit biasing
results.rationale: Microsoft Guidance Controlled Decoding 2026 documents the production-deployment instance of decoder-level constraint masks + per-token logit biasing — the engineering substrate on which the candidate's -inf-bias-by-default + conditional-release is trivially expressed. Guidance's mask-application-per-step + state-conditional unmask IS the candidate's logit_bias(t∈T_rare) = -inf + G(h_t)>τ release. Functional-twin on the constraint-mask + conditional-unmask architecture; semantic-twin on the controlled-decoding logit-biasing framing. The candidate's mechanism is a Guidance-style mask program with a learned predicate G(h_t) as the unmask condition — no architectural novelty beyond Guidance's existing programmatic-constraint API.

results.title: Vellum Logit Bias — logit-bias -100 ban / +5 boost mainstream parameter
results.rationale: Vellum Logit Bias confirms that the -inf/large-negative logit-bias suppression of a token catalog is a mainstream production parameter (logit_bias = -100 effectively bans; +5 boosts) — the candidate's logit_bias(t∈T_rare) = -inf is industry-standard. The candidate's only step beyond Vellum is making the bias state-conditional via G(h_t), which is exactly what production stacks (Guidance/Outlines/JSONFormer) already do by dynamically updating the logit_bias dict per step. Functional-twin on the -inf-bias = ban primitive; semantic-twin on logit-bias parameterization framing. The candidate's T_rare catalog is a user-supplied token list — exactly the Vellum API surface.

results.title: Constrained Generation Deep Dive — masking unwanted token logits
results.rationale: Constrained Generation Deep Dive enumerates the masking-unwanted-token-logits paradigm of which the candidate's logit_bias(t∈T_rare) = -inf is a textbook instance, and describes state-conditional mask updates per decoding step. The candidate's emission gate G(h_t) is a learned mask-update predicate, which the deep-dive covers under 'dynamic constraint state'. Functional-twin on the per-step mask + state-conditional update; semantic-twin on the constrained-generation framing. The candidate exhibits no novel masking primitive beyond what the deep-dive catalogues.

results.title: SIGDIAL 2025 Discourse Markers — discourse-marker evaluation across languages
results.rationale: SIGDIAL 2025 Discourse Markers supplies the cross-lingual discourse-marker evaluation substrate — the empirical regime in which the candidate's [CLICK_FEEDBACK]/[CERTAINTY]/[REFUSAL] markers would be evaluated. The marker-catalog framing is already standardized at SIGDIAL with evaluation protocols for position-sensitive marker emission; the candidate's contribution is to that framing. Semantic-twin on discourse-marker-token cataloging; functional-twin on marker-position evaluation. The candidate's discourse-back-channel feedback markers are precisely the marker class SIGDIAL 2025 evaluates.

reason: All 8 results trigger hit under sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7. The candidate decomposes exhaustively across four prior-art clusters: (cluster A — core logit-suppression + suppression-release mechanism) Uncovering Logit Suppression 2405.13068 (sem 0.90, func 0.90) IS the per-token logit-bias = -inf for a target-token catalog + position-conditional suppression-release that the candidate names; the candidate's T_rare suppression + G(h_t)>τ release is a relabeling. (cluster B — per-step attention-gated sanctioned-position predictor) Decoding-Time Debiasing PRM 2605.02348 (sem 0.88, func 0.88) supplies a per-step Bias-Guard gate firing only at sanctioned positions = the candidate's G(h_t)=sigmoid(W_gate·h_t) attention-gated emission predictor. (cluster C — discourse/backchannel marker installation) Backchannel/Filler FT 2509.20237 (sem 0.86, func 0.84) installs backchannel/filler marker tokens via FT on LLaMA-3 with position-conditional emission, AND Aligning Backchannel wBajQcrGEv (sem 0.82, func 0.80) supplies the contrastive-FT pipeline for joint dialogue+backchannel embedding = the candidate's optional contrastive FT for gate G; SIGDIAL 2025 Discourse Markers (sem 0.76, func 0.72) supplies the discourse-marker-token evaluation regime. (cluster D — production logit-bias + controlled-decoding substrate) Microsoft Guidance Controlled Decoding 2026 (sem 0.84, func 0.82) provides the constraint-mask + per-token logit-bias programmable substrate, Vellum Logit Bias (sem 0.80, func 0.78) confirms -inf-bias = ban is a mainstream API parameter, and Constrained Generation Deep Dive (sem 0.78, func 0.74) covers state-conditional dynamic mask updates. The three claimed contributions — (a) T_rare rare-marker catalog, (b) per-step attention-gated emission gate G(h_t), (c) -inf logit-suppression with conditional release — are anticipated cluster-by-cluster: (a) by Backchannel/Filler 2509.20237 + Aligning Backchannel wBajQcrGEv + SIGDIAL 2025; (b) by Decoding-Time Debiasing PRM 2605.02348 sanctioned-position gate; (c) by Uncovering Logit Suppression 2405.13068 explicit suppression+release analysis + Guidance + Vellum + Constrained Generation Deep Dive. The Hadzabe click-language / click-consonant / ejective-prenasalized cultural framing supplies only narrative for the rare-symbol-marker-in-discourse framing; the underlying mechanism is fully covered. total_hits = 8 >> 1, distinct_effect_clusters = 4 >> 1, candidate FAILS.
recommendation: REJECT candidate. HADZA-CLICK-ATTEN is fully anticipated by the conjunction Uncovering Logit Suppression 2405.13068 + Decoding-Time Debiasing PRM 2605.02348 + Backchannel/Filler 2509.20237 + Aligning Backchannel wBajQcrGEv, with Microsoft Guidance 2026 + Vellum + Constrained Generation Deep Dive supplying the production logit-bias substrate and SIGDIAL 2025 supplying the discourse-marker evaluation protocol. Specifically: (1) the -inf logit_bias(t∈T_rare) by default + suppression-release is the exact mechanism analyzed in 2405.13068 (suppression-release of catalog tokens), and is the trivial composition of Vellum's -100 ban + Guidance's per-step mask + Constrained Generation Deep Dive's state-conditional unmask; (2) the per-step attention-gated emission predictor G(h_t)=sigmoid(W_gate·h_t) over hidden state with threshold τ_emit is the Bias-Guard sanctioned-position gate of 2605.02348 under a different label; (3) the T_rare = {[CLICK_FEEDBACK], [STOP_EMIT], [CERTAINTY], [REFUSAL]} discourse-marker catalog is the backchannel/filler marker token catalog installed by 2509.20237's FT pipeline and aligned by wBajQcrGEv's contrastive embedding, with evaluation protocols at SIGDIAL 2025; (4) the optional contrastive FT of G on dialogue corpora with explicit marker positions is the wBajQcrGEv joint-embedding training objective. To salvage, the candidate would need a feature uniquely supplied by the Hadzabe click-language source not anticipated by these clusters, e.g.: (a) a 'click-phonemic register' constraint encoded as a co-articulation-coupled marker set where emitting [CLICK_FEEDBACK] at step t TRANSFORMS the suppression catalog at t+1..t+k (mimicking the prosodic afterglow of click phonemes that re-tunes neighboring phonetic contexts), giving a Markov-state-conditional suppression policy beyond Bias-Guard's i.i.d. sanctioned-position gate; (b) an 'ejective + prenasalized + click' tri-mode emission policy where each marker class has its own gate G_mode(h_t) and emissions are mutually-exclusive via softmax-with-suppress, with a formal information-theoretic lower bound on the marker-rate that 2405.13068's flat suppression catalog cannot express; (c) a 'language-isolate calibration' constraint that requires the gate to be tuned on a corpus-isolate (no transfer from related dialogues) with provable in-context-only adaptation — defeating the contrastive-FT pipeline of wBajQcrGEv which assumes related-dialogue transfer; (d) a 'discourse back-channel turn-yielding' operationalized as a gate G_yield(h_t) coupled to dialogue-state with a guaranteed pause-emission property bounded by sticky-HMM dynamics, with formal yield-rate convergence not addressed by SIGDIAL 2025 evaluation. As stated, HADZA-CLICK-ATTEN is in the saturated region documented by 2405.13068 + 2605.02348 + 2509.20237 + wBajQcrGEv + Guidance + Vellum + Constrained Generation Deep Dive + SIGDIAL 2025, with each of the three claimed contributions anticipated by at least two prior-art clusters.
